name: Dockerfile and dependency updates
description: Verify and update the Docker image to support the certs tool. The CT service uses HTTP (urllib) and TLS service uses ssl stdlib, so no new system packages are needed. Add any new Python dependencies to pyproject.toml if required. Build and verify the image runs the certs subcommand successfully.
acceptance_criteria: docker build -t stackshield . succeeds without errors
docker run --rm stackshield python apps/certs/certs.py --help prints usage without errors
pyproject.toml includes any new Python dependencies if they were added by prior features
No unnecessary packages were added to the Dockerfile
notes: Verified: no Dockerfile or pyproject.toml changes needed. The certs tool uses only Python stdlib (urllib, ssl, asyncio) plus pydantic (already a dependency). The Dockerfile already includes ca-certificates for TLS verification. Docker build/run could not be tested in this environment due to Docker access restrictions, but code-level review confirms no changes are required.
